author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
mama mana mandire raha niśidina
kṛṣṇa⋅murāri śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅murāri

bhakti⋅prīti⋅mālā⋅candana
tumi nio he nio cittānandana

jīvana maraṇa tava pūjā nivedana
sundara he manahārī

esa nandakumāra āra nandakumāra
habe premapradīpe ārati tomāra

nayane jamunā jhare anivāra
tomāra virahe giridhārī

vandana gāne tava bajuka jīvana
kṛṣṇa⋅murāri śrī⋅kṛṣṇa⋅murāri
en_translation:
In the temple of my mind, remain night and day, O Kṛṣṇa, the enemy of the demon Mura!

May You accept my garlands of devotion and love, and my sandalwood paste, O Lord who delights the heart.

My life and death are my worshipful offerings to You, O beautiful Lord who captivates the mind.

Come, O son of Nanda! Your ceremony will be performed with the lamp of love.

From my eyes, tears flow incessantly like the Yamunā in separation from You, O lifter of Govardhana Hill.

Let my life resound with songs of Your praise, O Lord who destroyed the demon Mura!
